rows.requirement: Business profile MUST be served over HTTPS.
rows.notes: Profile is the business well-known profile document.

rows.requirement: Profile endpoints MUST NOT use 3xx redirects; verifiers MUST NOT follow them.
rows.notes: Companion impl rule at overview.md#L1099: 'Implementations MUST NOT follow redirects (3xx) on profile fetches.' Browser fetch auto-follows redirects opaquely -> CLI-only to assert no-redirect.

rows.requirement: Profile responses MUST send Cache-Control with public and max-age>=60, and MUST NOT use private/no-store/no-cache.
rows.quote: Profile responses **MUST** include a `Cache-Control` header with `public` and `max-age` of at least 60 seconds. Profiles **MUST NOT** be served with `private`, `no-store`, or `no-cache` directives.
rows.notes: Reading response headers cross-origin is CORS-restricted in browser -> CLI-only.

rows.requirement: Implementations MUST reject profile URLs not served over HTTPS.
rows.quote: Implementations **MUST** reject profile URLs not served over HTTPS.
rows.notes: Tester-side behavior we also implement in our own profile fetcher.

rows.requirement: service endpoint MUST be a valid HTTPS URL.
rows.quote: `endpoint` **MUST** be a valid URL with scheme (https)
rows.notes: Official suite protocol_test asserts a non-null rest endpoint.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST include their profile URI in every request.
rows.quote: Platforms **MUST** include their profile URI in [every request via the UCP-Agent mechanism]
rows.notes: REST: UCP-Agent header (RFC 8941 dictionary, profile=). This is the platform/agent obligation; the tester IS the platform and must send it. Distinct from the 01-23/suite header set (request-signature/request-id are NOT 04-08 spec headers).
